title: In the case of using the role, there is a problem with the acl check of the view
poc: CREATE USER 'user1'@'localhost' IDENTIFIED WITH mysql_native_password BY 'pass';

GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, DROP, RELOAD, FILE, REFERENCES, INDEX, ALTER, SHOW DATABASES, CREATE TEMPORARY TABLES, LOCK TABLES, EXECUTE, CREATE VIEW, SHOW VIEW, CREATE ROUTINE, ALTER ROUTINE, CREATE USER, EVENT, TRIGGER, CREATE TABLESPACE, CREATE ROLE, DROP ROLE, ROLE_ADMIN ON *.* TO 'user1'@'localhost';
CREATE TABLE tt (id INT UNSIGNED AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, aa VARCHAR(10));
CREATE VIEW t11 AS SELECT tt.id AS id, tt.aa AS aa FROM tt;
key_bug_features: User lacks privileges to access created view
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, username VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, email VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE VIEW active_users AS SELECT id, username FROM users WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL 30 DAY;
